title: Address an Envelope Correctly
summary: Write a complete, correctly formatted address on an envelope.
learnerPromise: You will be able to write a mailing address in the correct format so the postal service can deliver it.
whyItMatters: A clearly addressed envelope reaches its destination faster and avoids being returned. Addressing mail correctly is a basic skill for sending letters, forms, payments, and packages.
realLifeUses: You need to mail a letter or payment to a business or government office
You are sending a birthday card or thank-you note to a relative
A form asks you to enclose a self-addressed stamped envelope
You need to return a document by mail and want it to arrive safely
materialsNeeded: The complete address you are mailing to
result: An envelope with a correctly formatted recipient address and your return address in the proper places.
youWillLearn: Where to write the recipient's address on the envelope
Where to write your return address
How to format a full address with name, street, city, state, and zip code
How to double-check an address for accuracy before mailing
miniChallenge: Write a complete, correctly formatted address on an envelope, including your return address in the proper place.
steps: Write your return address in the top-left corner of the envelope
Write the recipient's full name on the first line in the center
Write the street address or P.O. box on the second line
Write the city, state abbreviation, and zip code on the third line
Check that the writing is clear and all parts of both addresses are included
completionCriteria: The recipient's address is complete and written in the center of the envelope
Your return address is written in the top-left corner
commonProblems: The address is hard to read — use a dark pen and print clearly
You do not know the zip code — look it up online or ask the recipient
You forgot the return address — always include it so mail can come back if undeliverable
tips: Use all capital letters if your regular handwriting is hard to read
Do not write below the bottom inch of the envelope — that space is for postal barcodes
If mailing internationally, write the country name on the last line in capital letters
